h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: Tail's drawn here though — not down at the wheels.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply identify the interacting objects and place the corresponding label cards on the diagram.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [glances at down arrow] Earth on cart — foot on the chip too. But Earth doesn't touch... no washer for that one.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner actively applies the new representational criteria to a non-contact force, demonstrating a productive actional breakthrough.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The touch card settled who pushes. It did not settle why the foot sits here. [places a washer at the wheels and a blue chip on the cart body, beside the arrow tail] Two marks now. Washer: contact place. Blue chip: object receiving the force.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly takes stock of the prior route's failure and introduces a novel physical representation to separate the contact point from the force application point.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Two marks now. Washer: contact place. Blue chip: object receiving the force. Track card to the washer. Up-arrow name to the blue chip: force on cart.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is precise, physically separates the conflated concepts, and provides a highly actionable criterion for the learner to map the diagram.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The transcript ends immediately after the learner's pivot turn, leaving no subsequent tutor turn to evaluate for contingent uptake.
